item.title: Tour de France 2026 young rider classification favorites: The battle for white is more open than ever
item.description: The Tour de France always showcases the sport’s biggest young talents, making the fight for the white jersey one to watch closely. With Tadej Pogacar and UAE Emirates-XRG no longer eligible since 2024, the classification is wide open again.

item.description: MAAP has entered the eyewear market with its Advanced Optics range. The launch includes three models priced between 185 and 220 euros, featuring TR90 frames and interchangeable lenses.

item.description: Over nearly a century, the Tour de France advertising caravan has grown alongside the race and become an essential part of its economic success and popular appeal.

item.description: Twenty years after his Tour de France victory, Óscar Pereiro looks back on his career and takes on difficult comparisons with other riders from his era.

item.title: Tour de France 2026 mountains classification favorites: Potential Pogacar challengers are fading away
item.description: The polka-dot jersey remains one of cycling’s most iconic prizes. As the Tour de France approaches, the list of realistic challengers to Tadej Pogacar for the mountains classification appears to be shrinking.

item.title: Óscar Pereiro tests his memory of 2006, 20 years after his Tour win
item.description: Twenty years after the Tour that changed his life, Óscar Pereiro looks back at 2006 to see how much he remembers from that unforgettable year. From Barcelona’s Champions League title and Italy’s World Cup win to Fabio Cannavaro’s Ballon d’Or, Spain’s basketball world title, and the seasons of Nadal, Federer, Alonso and the Miami Heat, the Galician takes on MARCA’s quiz with memories, doubts and a few amusing mistakes. He even gets one cycling answer wrong, thinking Valverde won that year when it was Vinokourov.

item.title: Óscar Pereiro takes on a Tour de France mountain pass quiz 17 years after retirement
item.description: The 2026 Tour de France will climb some of the most iconic passes in the history of the Grande Boucle. Can 2006 winner Óscar Pereiro still recognize them?

item.description: The green jersey has long been the prize for the Tour de France’s best and most versatile sprinter. Last year, Tadej Pogacar nearly broke that trend, prompting a response from A.S.O. In de Leiderstrui reviews the main contenders for the 2026 points classification.

item.title: Michael Boogerd explains why this Tour will still be exciting despite Tadej Pogacar
item.description: Michael Boogerd will work the full Tour de France this year as a TV commentator for NOS. He will form the regular live commentary duo on Dutch television with Andries Lamain and will also appear five times on De Avondetappe. In an interview, Boogerd explains why he is still looking forward to an exciting Tour despite Tadej Pogacar’s presence.

item.title: BEAT lose 23-year-old Belgian Michiel Coppens to Cofidis
item.description: Michiel Coppens will turn professional with Cofidis after 2026. The 23-year-old Belgian, who finished in the top 10 at the Belgian Championships last week, is leaving BEAT Cycling.

item.title: OCNC game: the most interesting riders by price range
item.description: It is difficult to find hidden picks among these riders. Tadej Pogacar starts as the favourite as he chases a fifth overall victory.

item.title: Caruso to Seixas: forget the money
item.description: The veteran has sent a clear message to the rising talent.

item.title: TotalEnergies' Alan Jousseaume sidelined with mononucleosis
item.description: Alan Jousseaume is taking a break from racing to recover from mononucleosis.

item.title: Mads Pedersen still dreams of green jersey despite unfavourable points system
item.description: After being left out in favour of Jonathan Milan last year, Mads Pedersen is set to return to the Tour de France. The Dane will chase stage wins and target the green jersey, even though the new points system does not favour him.

item.title: Mads Pedersen targets Tour de France stage wins and the green jersey despite points changes
item.description: Mads Pedersen is back for the Tour de France after being overlooked last year in favor of Jonathan Milan. The Dane will chase stage wins over the coming weeks and also target the green jersey, even though the new points system does not favor him.

item.title: Damiano Caruso has one last Tour de France dream before retirement
item.description: Damiano Caruso says he still has one final Tour de France ambition before retiring.

item.title: Michiel Coppens to turn professional with Cofidis next year
item.description: Belgian rider Michiel Coppens will turn professional with Cofidis next year.

item.title: Célia Gery to make Tour de France Femmes debut in 2026
item.description: Célia Gery will ride the Tour de France Femmes for the first time in 2026.

item.title: Célia Gery to make Tour de France Femmes debut in 2026
item.description: Célia Gery will ride the Tour de France Femmes for the first time in 2026.

item.title: Poll: Who will win the 2026 Tour de France?
item.description: The 2026 Tour de France is expected to feature another star-studded field. Tadej Pogacar starts as the rider to beat as the two-time defending champion chases a fifth overall title, while Jonas Vingegaard is again among the leading contenders.

item.title: YOSOYCICLISTA campaign invites riders to join the cycling community
item.category: Real Federación Española de Ciclismo – RFEC
item.description: The YOSOYCICLISTA campaign encourages cyclists to share their motivation and story and join the cycling community.

item.title: Tour de France is a mental test, not just a numbers game
item.description: Per Bausager says the Tour de France will reveal more than what can be calculated on paper, highlighting the race as a mental test as much as a physical one.

item.title: Official Ride The Route sportives announced ahead of Lloyds Tour of Britain
item.description: Cyclists will be able to ride parts of two stages of the Lloyds Tour of Britain route in two official Ride The Route sportives.

item.title: Netcompany Ineos target Tour de France stages with strong opening team time trial lineup
item.description: Netcompany Ineos are heading to the Tour de France focused mainly on stage wins, with the opening team time trial offering their first major opportunity. The British team brings a powerful time trial squad to Barcelona, including Filippo Ganna, Joshua Tarling, Tobias Foss, Thymen Arensman, Dorian Godon, Kévin Vauquelin, Egan Bernal and Michał Kwiatkowski.

item.title: Marceli Boguslawski wins both opening half-stages of Course Cycliste de Solidarnosc et des Champions Olympiques
item.description: Course Cycliste de Solidarnosc et des Champions Olympiques (2.2) opened with two half-stages. Both ended in bunch sprints, and both were won by ATT Investments sprinter Marceli Boguslawski after strong team performances. With three days still to race, the Polish rider leads the yellow and white jersey classifications, while the Czech team tops the team standings. The two wins raise ATT Investments' season total in UCI races to nine and add 16 more UCI points.

item.title: Lidl-Trek balance Juan Ayuso's podium bid with Mads Pedersen's green jersey goal at Tour de France
item.description: Lidl-Trek head into this year's Tour de France with major ambitions on several fronts. The team wants to guide Juan Ayuso to the podium in Paris, while also chasing stage wins and the points classification with Mads Pedersen. The question is whether both goals can work within the same team.

item.title: Tadej Pogacar unveils new Richard Mille watch linked to Colnago collaboration
item.description: Tadej Pogacar, already often seen wearing a Richard Mille watch, is now also an ambassador for the brand. The Slovenian is fronting a new collaboration between Richard Mille and Colnago, the bike supplier of UAE Emirates XRG.

item.title: Visma | Lease a Bike honors Vingegaard's Triple Crown with special commemorative pin
item.description: Before the start of the Tour de France in Barcelona, Vingegaard received a special commemorative pin from CEO Richard Plugge. The tribute from Visma | Lease a Bike was presented as recognition of his Triple Crown.

item.title: Eddy Merckx says Tadej Pogacar would be welcome in exclusive five-time Tour de France winners club
item.description: If Tadej Pogacar wins his fifth Tour de France this month, he would join an exclusive group of riders as a joint record holder alongside names including Eddy Merckx. Speaking to Het Nieuwsblad, Merckx said Pogacar would be welcome in that club.

item.title: Eddy Merckx says Tadej Pogacar would be welcome in exclusive five-time Tour de France winners club
item.description: If Tadej Pogacar wins his fifth Tour de France this month, he would join an exclusive group of riders as a joint record holder alongside names including Eddy Merckx. Speaking to Het Nieuwsblad, Merckx said Pogacar would be welcome in that club.

item.title: Full routes announced for all five stages of the 2026 Tour of Britain Men
item.description: The full stage routes for the 2026 Tour of Britain Men have been announced. The race will cover 565 miles across five stages, starting in Lincolnshire and finishing in the Scottish Borders Lowlands.

item.title: Josh Tarling makes Tour de France debut three weeks after collarbone surgery
item.description: Three weeks after a crash left him with a broken collarbone and appeared to end his Tour de France hopes, Josh Tarling has recovered from surgery in time to make his debut in cycling's biggest race for Ineos.

item.title: Human rights groups call for UAE Team Emirates' UCI license to be suspended
item.description: Four human rights organizations are calling for UAE Team Emirates' UCI license to be suspended because of the conflict in Sudan.

item.title: What to expect from Julian Alaphilippe at the Tour de France
item.description: Julian Alaphilippe will start his eighth Tour de France on Saturday. The Tudor rider has won six stages in previous appearances, and stage victories are again the goal this year. The question is what he can deliver after a modest spring, although he says he feels good.

item.title: RCS Sport: Luca Guercilena set to join Giro d’Italia organizer’s staff
item.description: Luca Guercilena is expected to join the staff of RCS Sport, the organizer of the Giro d’Italia, from September.

item.title: Tour de France - Julian Alaphilippe: 'I’m still a bit old school'
item.description: Julian Alaphilippe says he still feels 'a bit old school' ahead of the Tour de France.

item.title: Tour de France - Julian Alaphilippe: 'I still love to suffer and push myself'
item.description: Julian Alaphilippe says he still enjoys suffering and pushing himself at the Tour de France.

item.title: Tour de France - Julian Alaphilippe: 'Passing on my experience matters to me'
item.description: Julian Alaphilippe says sharing his experience is important to him at the Tour de France.

item.title: Wout van Aert says recovery is going well and he is back riding
item.description: Wout van Aert will miss the Tour de France after an infected elbow wound delayed his recovery, but the Visma | Lease a Bike rider says his rehabilitation is going well and he is already back on the bike.

item.title: Retirement, the Giro miracle and Pogacar vs. Vingegaard - Domestique Hotseat with Damiano Caruso
item.description: A Domestique Hotseat podcast episode featuring Damiano Caruso on retirement, the Giro miracle and Pogacar vs. Vingegaard.

item.title: Ahead of the Tour de France, Pogacar shows his class off the bike too
item.description: Tadej Pogacar heads into the Tour de France as the clear favorite, and the UAE Team Emirates-XRG rider has also impressed off the bike by raising money and making a large donation himself for charity.

item.title: Aike Visbeek explains sudden departure: 'They are better off moving on immediately'
item.description: Aike Visbeek has responded to his immediate departure from Lotto-Intermarché, announced just four days before the Tour de France, saying the team is better off moving on right away.

item.title: Tour de France prepares for heat in the first week
item.description: The Tour de France could face extreme heat as early as the opening week.

item.title: Tour 2026: Six riders who can complete the Grand Tour stage-win trilogy
item.description: Six riders in the Tour de France 2026 peloton could complete the feat of winning a stage in all three Grand Tours.

item.title: Tour de France 2026: Six riders who can complete the Grand Tour stage-win trilogy
item.description: Six riders starting the Tour de France 2026 can complete the feat of winning a stage in all three Grand Tours. WielerFlits lists the contenders, including Pascal Ackermann (Jayco AlUla).

item.title: Report: Lidl-Trek boss takes new role
item.description: A Lidl-Trek boss is reportedly set for a senior position at sports and media company RCS Sport.

item.title: Tour de France 2026 route: all the climbs
item.description: The many climbs on the Tour de France 2026 route are expected to play a decisive role. The race covers 3,321.2 kilometres and 54,450 metres of elevation gain, with 69 categorized climbs and five summit finishes, including two stage finishes on Alpe d’Huez.

item.title: Tadej Pogacar unveils nearly €1 million watch but will not wear it at the Tour de France
item.description: Tadej Pogacar is the face of a new collaboration between Richard Mille and Colnago. They have created a watch worth just under €1 million, but Pogacar will not wear it during the Tour de France for safety reasons.

item.title: Tadej Pogacar gets nearly €1 million watch but will leave it at home for the Tour de France
item.description: Tadej Pogacar is the face of a new collaboration between Richard Mille and Colnago. Together they designed a watch worth just under €1 million, but Pogacar will not wear it during the Tour de France for safety reasons.

item.title: Remco Evenepoel spotted with prototype time trial bike for the Tour de France
item.description: Remco Evenepoel has been seen using a prototype time trial bike ahead of the Tour de France, raising questions about a possible update to the Specialized Shiv TT. A new Roval time trial wheel was also spotted at the Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe camp.

item.title: Tour de France special kits: Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe unveil alternate jersey
item.description: Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe have revealed a special Tour de France kit. Visma-Lease a Bike, Movistar, Pinarello-Q36.5 and Jayco AlUla also have special designs for July.

item.title: Julian Alaphilippe targets seventh Tour de France stage win
item.description: Julian Alaphilippe said a stage victory would be a dream as he aims for his seventh Tour de France stage win.

item.title: Eddy Merckx backs Tadej Pogačar for 2026 Tour de France victory
item.description: Eddy Merckx believes Tadej Pogačar can win the 2026 Tour de France and possibly become the first rider to take a sixth title. The Belgian also said expectations should be kept in check for Paul Seixas in his first Tour.

item.title: TOUR Transalp 2026 ends at Lake Garda after seven stages
item.description: Riders from 37 nations completed TOUR Transalp 2026 over 750 kilometres and 17,000 metres of climbing from Lienz to Riva del Garda. The event finished after seven stages at Lake Garda.

item.title: Bahamontes’ prediction about Contador came true the last time the Tour de France visited Barcelona
item.description: Barcelona will host the Tour de France for the fourth time. The last visit came in 2009, in a stage remembered for a notable backstory involving Bahamontes and Contador.

item.title: Cofidis signs in-form Belgian continental rider
item.description: He described the move as a dream come true.

item.title: Mathieu van der Poel sees LA2028 as a possible end point for his career
item.description: Mathieu van der Poel is not yet thinking concretely about retirement, but the Dutch all-rounder is looking more and more toward the final chapters of his career.

item.title: The 1996 Tour de France win by Riis and Telekom changed cycling — where is that squad 30 years later?
item.description: The German team stunned the peloton three decades ago by ending Miguel Indurain's dominance at the Tour de France. This looks at where Bjarne Riis, Jan Ullrich and their teammates are now.

item.title: Tour de France 2026 stage preview with Jens Voigt
item.description: Jens Voigt analyzes the opening stages in Barcelona and the mountain stages in the Pyrenees and Massif Central.

item.title: BEAT leader Michiel Coppens to join Cofidis in 2027
item.description: Michiel Coppens will ride for Cofidis from 2027. The 23-year-old Belgian currently races for the continental team BEAT cc p/b Saxo and finished tenth in the Belgian national road race championships last weekend. Coppens has been with the Dutch continental team since 2025 and was a trainee for several months in 2024.

item.description: Between his withdrawal from the Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes in mid-June and his arrival in Barcelona on Tuesday for the Tour de France start on Saturday, Paul Seixas went through a somewhat unsettled two-and-a-half-week transition period.

item.title: The tragic story of Dmytro Grabovskyy
item.description: Dmytro Grabovskyy, the 2005 Under-23 world champion once tipped as a future star, never lived up to the huge expectations. Struggling with addiction, the Ukrainian rider, once presented as the new Jan Ullrich, died in 2017 at the age of 31.

item.title: Giant and Liv introduce their fastest and lightest gravel bikes yet
item.description: Giant and Liv have introduced new gravel bikes: the Giant Revolt Advanced SL and the Liv Devote Advanced SL. The Taiwanese brand says they are the lightest and fastest gravel bikes it has ever produced. The new models were previously seen as prototypes at Unbound Gravel.

item.description: Mathieu van der Poel, now 31 and heading to the Tour de France as Alpecin-Premier Tech leader, is still at his peak. But the prospect that he could stop racing in as little as two and a half years is a reminder to appreciate him while he is still competing.

item.description: Miguel Indurain spoke to Eurosport ahead of this week’s Tour de France, discussing Tadej Pogacar, Juan Ayuso and Seixas. He said Pogacar is the clear favourite, but not without rivals.

item.title: Lenny Martinez targets a Tour de France stage win or polka-dot jersey
item.description: After a strong and consistent start to the season, Lenny Martinez of Bahrain Victorious will have freedom at the Tour de France to chase a stage victory or the mountains classification after two difficult editions in 2024 and 2025.

item.title: UAE Belgian ready to dominate with Pogacar in the Tour de France: 'The hardest fitness session of my life'
item.description: At UAE Emirates-XRG, everyone is ready to go all in with Tadej Pogacar in the Tour de France. The Slovenian has been outstanding all year and is expected to be even better in July, backed by seven top-level teammates including Tim Wellens.

item.title: Tour 2026: Belgium has never had so much sprinting depth
item.description: Fans hoping to see Belgian success in the Tour de France should mark the flat stages. Belgium has an exceptional group of fast riders, with five sprint opportunities on paper in this Tour de France.

item.title: Leaving Visma has not yet brought Valter success, but it has given him insight into Tour de France leaders Martinez and Tiberi
item.description: Attila Valter hoped his move from Visma | Lease a Bike to Bahrain Victorious would let him race for himself again, but the first half of the season has not gone to plan. The 28-year-old Hungarian will miss the Tour de France, though he has gained insight into team leaders Martinez and Tiberi.

item.title: Unconventional but effective: NSN's Plan B for Biniam Girmay, including his sprint train, for the Tour de France
item.description: Biniam Girmay surprisingly won three stages and the green jersey in the Tour de France two years ago. After an 18-month dry spell, the Eritrean has shown at NSN that he is back on track. What can be expected from him and his team in this Tour de France?

item.title: Tim van Dijke overcomes extra setback on road to Tour de France debut
item.description: Saturday 4 July will mark a new milestone in Tim van Dijke’s career. After the Giro d'Italia and Vuelta a Espana, he will make his Tour de France debut, following brother Mick. He is set to ride in support of Remco Evenepoel after earning the team’s full confidence in just a few months.

item.title: Milan Fretin gets full lead-out support for Tour de France
item.description: Jasper Philipsen (Alpecin-Premier Tech) and Tim Merlier (Soudal Quick-Step) are among the strongest sprinters in the Tour de France, but Belgian fast man Milan Fretin (Cofidis) is also targeting success. After knocking on the door for several years, Fretin now has his team’s full backing and will be supported by a complete lead-out train.

item.title: Bert Van Lerberghe on Tour de France sprint chances and the attacker Soudal Quick-Step wants to control
item.description: The Tour de France 2026 starts on Saturday in Barcelona, but the sprinters may have to wait until stage five for their first real opportunity. With a demanding opening weekend in Catalonia and the transfer into France via the Pyrenees, riders like Tim Merlier will first need to get through the early kilometers. Lead-out man Bert Van Lerberghe discussed the limited sprint chances and the attacking rider Soudal Quick-Step does not want to let go.

item.title: Lars Craps goes from long rehabilitation to Tour de France debut
item.description: Lars Craps earned a Tour de France selection after a very consistent Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, riding in the shadow of Wout van Aert, Maxim Van Gils and Cian Uijtdebroeks. In Saint-Amand-Montrond he finished eighth from the breakaway, and in Crest-Voland he placed 13th after a long day out front.

item.title: Asgreen and Vandborg agree on what will decide the Tour team time trial
item.description: Constant speed, perfect turns and a united team. Brian Vandborg and Kasper Asgreen explain what it takes to win the opening stage team time trial of the Tour.

item.title: White jersey game: who can challenge Paul Seixas? Evenepoel is no longer eligible
item.description: The white jersey competition remains open, with Paul Seixas among the leading names. As a reminder, the rider wearing the white jersey earns 5 points for each day he finishes in it, while Remco Evenepoel is no longer eligible for the classification.

item.title: Tour 2026 preview: favorites for the mountains classification
item.description: Predicting the winner of the mountains classification is difficult, but that only makes it more interesting. General classification contenders can target the iconic polka-dot jersey, while attacking riders will also hope to take it. WielerFlits looks ahead at the main favorites.

item.title: ‘Pogacar seems better supported than Vingegaard’: does UAE have the edge over Visma at the Tour de France?
item.description: An analysis of whether UAE arrives at the Tour de France with a stronger supporting team around Tadej Pogacar than Visma does around Jonas Vingegaard.

item.title: Teammate says Pogacar’s level is ‘downright depressing’
item.description: A teammate described Pogacar’s current level as ‘downright depressing,’ underlining just how dominant he appears ahead of the Tour de France.

item.description: On Saturday, 25 October 2003, the Roda JC stadium in Kerkrade hosted the finals of the Rabo Dikke Banden Races instead of football. It was one of the first times Mathieu van der Poel was seen in action in a cycling event, where he received his first prize from Michael Boogerd.

Van der Poel says he loves and hates the Tour before the Grand Départ in Barcelona
item.description: Mathieu van der Poel is one of the best Classics riders of all time, but his relationship with Grand Tours is more complicated. He has ridden the Tour de France five times, won two stages and worn the yellow jersey in two editions, but finished it only twice. He now says he appreciates the Tour more as he gets older and is considering ending his racing career after 2028.

item.description: Tommaso Dati is the new leader of the Coppa Italia delle Regioni 2026. After finishing second in the road race at the Italian Championships, the Team UKYO rider moved to the top of the individual standings with 180 points, ahead of Giulio Pellizzari on 164. Davide Donati is third on 110, followed by Diego Ulissi on 109 and Federico Iacomoni on 104.

item.title: Magnus Cort Nielsen and Amalie Dideriksen headline Danish championship weekend
item.description: Denmark crowned national champions across road, time trial, mountain bike and trial disciplines over the weekend. On the road, Magnus Cort Nielsen won the elite men’s road race and Amalie Dideriksen took the elite women’s title. In the time trials, Kristian Egholm won the elite men’s crown and Sif Nikoline Bendix Madsen claimed the elite women’s title. In mountain bike, Sofie Heby Pedersen and Rose-Mai Damsgaard each won two national titles in XCC and XCO, while Gustav Heby Pedersen won the elite men’s XCO title and Sebastian Fini Juul took XCC gold.

With Remco Evenepoel gone, the Belgian team returns to a sprint-centered lineup built around Tim Merlier, supported by Pascal Eenkhoorn, Dylan van Baarle, Bert Van Lerberghe and Jasper Stuyven. Mikel Landa is absent, while Ilan Van Wilder will aim for the general classification.

Why Alberto Monti faded at the national championships, and why ATT Investments were not celebrating on the podium
item.description: Alberto Monti won the men's elite time trial, the only senior men's national title claimed by a Czech team. In the road race, he was the only rider to go with the early favorites from foreign teams, but after halfway he began to drop back and finished last among those who completed the race. Riders from ATT Investments reached the finish at the very front, yet had little reason to celebrate. Three podium places from the two elite races were no substitute for a single national title. Their consolation is ATT Investments' position in the UCI Europe Tour ranking, where they sit second.

item.title: Milan, Grégoire and Herregodts take major national titles as Kelderman also celebrates
item.description: National championships were also held across the rest of Europe over the weekend. UAE Emirates-XRG enjoyed a particularly successful weekend with titles in Italy, Switzerland and Portugal, while Lidl-Trek triumphed in the USA, Italy and the Czech Republic.

item.title: Tour de France debutants Paul Seixas and Olav Kooij to share Decathlon CMA CGM support as Gall misses out
item.description: Decathlon CMA CGM will not rely only on rising star Paul Seixas at this year's Tour de France. Sprinter Olav Kooij will also make his debut in the race after joining the French team with the Tour in mind. The question is whether the rest of the Decathlon lineup can support both ambitious riders.

item.title: Michael Kukrle was the top performer from the domestic conti scene at the national championships
item.description: At the national championships, Mathias Vacek again showed he is currently the strongest Czech rider, while the other elite podium finishers also underlined their quality. Looking beyond that top level, Michael Kukrle was the best rider from the Czech continental scene. He finished fifth in both the time trial and the road race. Those results reflect his strong season so far, which has made him the highest-ranked Czech rider at conti level in the UCI rankings.

item.description: Tadej Pogačar is the clear favorite for this year’s Tour de France, but he will not win a fifth title without a strong team. UAE Emirates-XRG has named seven domestiques for this year, including Isaac del Toro and Adam Yates. Pavel Sivakov did not make the lineup.

item.title: Magnus Cort announces retirement, then wins first Danish title ahead of Tour de France
item.description: Magnus Cort has had a remarkable week. After being selected by Uno-X Mobility for the Tour de France, getting engaged, and announcing he will retire after this season, the 33-year-old then won the Danish national title for the first time in his career.

item.title: Shari Bossuyt beats Lotte Kopecky to win Belgian title in women’s road race
Shari Bossuyt wins first Belgian road title after comeback
item.description: Shari Bossuyt (25, AG Insurance-Soudal) won her first Belgian road title in Brasschaat, beating Lotte Kopecky in the sprint. Bossuyt was emotional after the finish. After a suspension, she returned to winning ways with victory in the GP de Wallonie and a stage win in the Vuelta.

item.title: Zoe Backstedt completes British double with elite women’s road race win
item.description: Zoe Backstedt won the British elite women’s road race in Aberystwyth to complete a double after her time trial victory.

item.title: Wilco Kelderman wins Dutch national title after 11-year wait
item.description: Wilco Kelderman ended more than 11 years without a win at the Dutch National Championships. The 35-year-old finally won again and will wear the red, white and blue jersey.

item.title: Shriever finishes fifth as GBCT BMX racers have mixed fortunes in Sarrians
item.description: The Great Britain Cycling Team BMX racing squad gave everything at the 2026 UEC BMX Racing European Championships in Sarrians, France, with mixed results across the eight-rider team.

item.title: KNWU and JOIN join forces for recreational cyclists
item.description: The KNWU and JOIN are working closely together to better support recreational cyclists in the Netherlands with training, development, and sporting goals. Through this long-term partnership, the cycling federation is combining its knowledge, network, and expertise with one of the leading training platforms for cyclists.

item.title: Cyclists face more crashes ahead of the holidays, BESIP, Dám respekt and Wolt remind road users to be careful
item.description: With warmer weather and the holidays approaching, more cyclists and scooter riders are out on the streets and roads, and crashes involving them are also increasing. Representatives of BESIP, the Czech Ministry of Transport, the Dám respekt initiative and technology company Wolt highlighted the need to follow the rules, keep a safe distance and show greater consideration among all road users at Wednesday’s awareness event, Den respektu, on Prague’s Ludvík Svoboda embankment.

item.title: Mathias Vacek says it was very hard today as he wins a third national title
item.description: The Czech and Slovak Championships in Jeseník ended with the expected victories for Mathias Vacek and Lukáš Kubiš. All the main contenders agreed that Vacek was a deserved winner of an exceptionally demanding championship. “I have to say it was really hard today. Right from the start eight of us got away, and we took turns well in the group, so the pace was very high, which was quite demanding,” Vacek said after the finish.

item.title: Mathias Vacek wins his third Czech title in Jeseník as Lukáš Kubiš also celebrates
item.description: Mathias Vacek confirmed his status as the main favorite and comfortably defended his title in the elite men’s road race at the Czech Championships in Jeseník. The Lidl-Trek rider sealed victory with an attack on the final climb before the finish and claimed his third national title. Tomáš Přidal (United Shipping) took second, while Jakub Otruba (Caja Rural-Seguros RGA) finished third. Lukáš Kubiš (Unibet Rose Rockets) became Slovak champion for the third year in a row.

item.title: Live coverage of the men’s race at the Czech and Slovak Championships in Jeseník
item.description: Follow the action from the hot Czech and Slovak Championships in Jeseník with us. Organizers moved the start of the 208-kilometer race 30 minutes earlier, but extreme conditions and a brutally hard day in the saddle are still expected.

item.title: After Remco Evenepoel, Tim Wellens also withdraws from Belgian Championships
item.description: Defending champion Tim Wellens has also withdrawn from the Belgian road championships on Sunday. Wellens was still on the provisional start list on Saturday, but decided not to race. Remco Evenepoel had already used a medical certificate to avoid the ‘mandatory’ Belgian Championships. Wout van Aert will also not start and, due to the after-effects of his elbow injury, will not ride the Tour de France either.

item.title: Heat, melting asphalt and referee intervention: Jeseník championship faces safety concerns before men’s race
item.description: Extreme heat has significantly affected the Czech and Slovak national championships in Jeseník. The women’s road race was shortened because of surface problems on Rejvíz, and organizers are now deciding how Sunday’s men’s race will look. Race director Leopold König says safety is the top priority. Mathias Vacek and Lukáš Kubiš expect a hard and attractive race, but both admit the condition of the course could play an important role.

item.title: Julia Kopecký wins Czech road title after sprinting past Zemanová
item.description: Julia Kopecký became Czech road race champion in Jeseník. In the sun-baked race, which officials shortened to three laps because of surface problems on Rejvíz, she beat Kristýna Zemanová in the sprint. Barbora Němcová took bronze. Viktória Chladoňová won the Slovak title, finishing just ahead of the Czech pair in the overall standings. Among junior women, Karolína Špicarová defended her national title.

item.title: 22-year-old Kristian Egholm wins Danish time trial title in major upset
item.description: There were several surprises at the Danish time trial championships. In the women’s race, triathlete Sif Nikoline Bendix Madsen won with a time of 37:08.58. In the men’s race, 22-year-old Kristian Egholm of Lidl-Trek Future Racing caused the biggest upset, winning in 33:20.54. Men’s top 3: Kristian Egholm (Lidl-Trek Future Racing) 33:20.54, Mikkel Norsgaard Bjerg (UAE Team Emirates-XRG) +0:38, Peter Øxenberg Hansen (INEOS Grenadiers) +5:10. Women’s top 3: Sif Nikoline Bendix Madsen (Aalborg Cykle-Ring) 37:08.58, Gertrud Riis Madsen (Team Aalborg Sparekassen Danmark Women) +9:36, Silje Weitze Antvorskov (Team FRIIS) +1:05.39.

item.description: The World Championships will be held on home soil in Huntsville, Alabama, from September 4-7, 2026.

item.title: Dutch mountain bike team heads to La Thuile World Cup with confidence
item.description: La Thuile is one of the new World Cup venues this season. The Italian village on the southeast side of the Mont Blanc massif has hosted regular mountain bike races in recent years, and from July 3 to 5 it will stage XCC and XCO races at the highest level. The Dutch mountain bike team is ready for it.

item.title: Vacek wins third straight national time trial title, with Otruba second and Kelemen third
item.description: There were no surprises at the national championships in Uničov. Mathias Vacek was clearly the best in the 48-kilometer time trial and claimed the elite title for the third year in a row. Jakub Otruba took his fifth straight medal, while Petr Kelemen finished third. In the Slovak race, Lukáš Kubiš defended his title.

item.title: Alberto Monti: 'I was worried about the extreme heat. It is easy to overheat. We did everything to prevent that. Everyone in the team is thrilled!'
item.description: Alberto Monti only began racing seriously when he moved into the elite ranks. He joined ATT Investments in mid-2024 at age 20, after barely racing as a junior. That may be one reason for his rapid progress. This year he has already collected strong results in France, Greece, Austria, Poland and at home, including the Peace Race U23. His victory in the national U23 time trial is, in his view, the biggest moment of his short career so far.

item.title: Drama in Uničov as Julia Kopecký defends national time trial title against Kvasničková, with Chladoňová best among Slovaks
item.description: Julia Kopecký won the Czech time trial championship for the third time in her career and defended her national title despite heavy pressure from Eliška Kvasničková. The VIF Cycling Team also had success in Uničov, winning four medals in the Czech and Slovak events. Viktória Chladoňová won the Slovak elite time trial as expected.

item.title: Monti wins U23 time trial title, with Strejček and Šumpík on the podium
item.description: Alberto Monti of ATT Investments is the new national U23 time trial champion. On the 48-kilometer course in Uničov, he had no rival and beat second-placed Adam Strejček by 1:14 to claim his first title.

item.title: Drama in Uničov as Julie Kopecký defends national time trial title against Kvasničková, with Chladoňová best among Slovaks
item.description: Julie Kopecký won the Czech time trial championship for the third time in her career and defended her national title despite heavy pressure from Eliška Kvasničková. The VIF Cycling Team also had success in Uničov, winning four medals in the Czech and Slovak events. Viktória Chladoňová won the Slovak elite time trial as expected.

item.title: Roman Kreuziger Cycling Academy celebrates a double as Daniel Kuběna becomes Czech junior champion and Timotej Michalík wins in Slovakia
item.description: The junior national time trial championships went as expected, but that did not make them any less dramatic. In the Czech race, Kuběna (RKCA) and Zaňka (Dukla) took the top two places, while another RKCA rider, Timotej Michalík, became Slovak champion. The 17-year-old showed strong form at the International Cycling Days at Ncup, but his best time may still have been a surprise. RKCA can be pleased with its work: all six Czech riders finished in the top 15, and all three Slovak riders finished in the top five.

item.title: Onley will miss the Tour de France after crash at Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes
item.description: Oscar Onley was set to lead Netcompany Ineos in the general classification at this year's Tour de France, but he will not be in the lineup. The fourth-placed rider from last year's Tour is still dealing with a shoulder injury, is not training as planned, and will not be able to start.

item.title: Jakub Zaňka and Daniel Kuběna are the favourites for the junior national time trial title
item.description: The joint Czech and Slovak national championships begin today with the individual time trials for men, women, U23 riders, junior women and junior men. The junior race has 82 entrants, including 23 Slovak and 59 Czech riders. The main favourites are Jakub Zaňka and Daniel Kuběna.

item.title: Kasper crypto4me targets medals at the national championships
item.description: Kasper crypto4me, the most successful team of recent years, heads into the national championships with medal ambitions. Last year, they were the only team besides Mathias Vacek to win at least two medals. Sport director Petr Kaltofen said a medal would be the main goal again, with active racing the priority.

item.title: Podcast: Huge elevation gain in Jeseníky

The Czech-Slovak edition will take place in Jeseník in the Czech Republic. It will be a race with a lot of climbing: eight laps in the Elite men’s category will give riders more than 4,000 meters of elevation gain, according to the organizers.

item.title: Denmark's biggest cycling stars gather for the Danish Championships during DIF DM-ugen
item.description: When DIF DM-ugen comes to Herning, the city will host the Danish road cycling championships for both women and men. On Friday and Sunday, some of the country's strongest riders will compete over two days of top-level national racing for the Danish titles. Friday features the time trial, with a 29.2-kilometer course starting and finishing at Torvet in Herning. The first rider starts at 3:30 p.m., with the U23 and elite women contesting the national titles before the men finish the day on Friday evening. Sunday brings the road race, traditionally one of the biggest national races of the year, with many of Denmark's leading international riders competing for the right to call themselves Danish champion. The start lists are strong, and spectators can look forward to seeing Danish top-level cycling up close as riders fight for medals, titles, and the right to wear the Dannebrog jersey for the coming year as reigning Danish champion. The Danish Championships are one of the most important recurring events in Danish cycling and a central part of DIF DM-ugen. The races also offer a unique chance to bring Danish cycling together and closer to the public. You can see start lists, times, and other relevant information here: Friday: https://www.sportstiming.dk/event/17431 Sunday: https://www.sportstiming.dk/event/17432

item.title: Shock before the national championships: women's defending champion Kristýna Burlová will not race in Jeseník
item.description: Czech champion Kristýna Burlová will not defend her title this year. One of the biggest stars of the domestic championships had to abandon both the time trial and, eventually, the road race after a crash in Spain. The national championships in Jeseník will therefore be without the reigning winner just as one of the toughest races in recent years is expected.

item.title: Vacek looks ahead to the home championships but expects no help
item.description: Mathias Vacek is widely expected to leave this year's Czech Republic championships in Uničov and Jeseník with both titles. He is the clear favorite in the time trial, and only bad luck is likely to stop him from winning. The road race will be more difficult, but as he showed last year, he can handle a complicated race even without team support.

item.title: Updated: What is the Jeseník national championships circuit like? Possibly easier than it looks
item.description: The organizers of the joint Czech and Slovak championships announced 4,000 meters of climbing, but maps show 3,360 meters. So what is the Jeseník national championships circuit like? Yes, it is hard, but Mathias Vacek will not have a guaranteed victory. The course does not rule out tactical advantages for larger teams, as has often been the case in the past.

item.title: Time for the European BMX Championships
item.description: The BMX season is well underway, and after two strong World Cups and a good European Cup in the spring, attention now turns to the European Championships in France. Danish riders have already shown well in the first four World Cup rounds, reaching the final in four races across categories. The European Championships will be held in Sarrians, which also hosted the first two World Cup rounds in early June. Malene Kejlstrup took her best World Cup result there with second place. National coach Klaus Bøgh Bonde says he is looking forward to the event and has selected the team based mainly on current form. The Danish team for the 2026 European Championships in Sarrians is: Elite women: Malene Kejlstrup; U23 women: Bolette Schlütter; Junior women: Ida Østergaard; Elite men: Magnus Dyhre, Jakob Sardag; U23 men: Jonas Idskov, Rasmus Simonsen; Junior men: William Garner, Jeppe Kejlstrup.

item.title: U23 Danish TT and DEMIN Cup in Skælskør: a major test against the wind
item.description: Skælskør hosted several major races on the same windy, sunlit course: the men's U23 Danish time trial championship and the second round of the DEMIN Cup for men and women. In the U23 race, Mads Landbo retained his title with an average speed of more than 52.11 km/h over 24.7 km. Kristian Egholm and Alfred Christensen completed the podium. In the women's DEMIN Cup time trial, Gertrud Riis Madsen won ahead of Alberte Greve and Julie Marckmann. Silje Weitze Antvorskov remains in the overall lead, while Ida Dam Fialla took the white youth jersey. In the men's DEMIN Cup, Ville Merløv kept the overall lead, with Mads Landbo and Mathias Larsen tied for second. The next round will be held on Bornholm on 22 August.

item.title: Michael Kukrle wins third Czech gravel cup round in Proseč after long jury debate
item.description: The winner of the third race in the Czech gravel cup was not decided until the sprint to the finish gate at Cyklo Maštale in Proseč on the Bohemian-Moravian Highlands. The result was then debated for almost an hour after the finish. Road rider Michael Kukrle (Kasper crypto4me) was declared the winner, while off-road specialist Filip Adel (Superior) was left disappointed.

item.title: Mountain bikes move to 32-inch wheels as Lenzerheide World Series confirms form

It also marked a further step back for Czech riders from their recent positions.

Fci president Dagnoni said safety for cyclists must become an absolute priority in Italy.

item.title: Mathias Enghuus appointed new mechanic for the Danish national team
item.description: Danmarks Cykle Union has hired Mathias Enghuus as mechanic for the Danish national team. He is already a familiar face at Danmarks Cykle Union after many years of volunteer work with the road national teams. His experience with DCU already includes the European Championships, World Championships and many trips with the U23 national team. Elite chief Morten Bennekou is pleased that Enghuus will join as full-time mechanic in August. Enghuus will take up the role on 1 August 2026.

item.title: Historic Fyn setting could host the World Championships
item.description: Denmark has submitted a bid to the UCI to host the 2031 Cyclocross World Championships, with Egeskov Slot on Fyn as the proposed venue. The bid is backed by Sport Event Denmark, Faaborg-Midtfyn Kommune and Destination Fyn, and highlights the setting around Egeskov Slot, which also hosts Heartland Festival. If successful, the event would bring about 300 cyclocross riders, 20,000 spectators and 300 accredited media to the area. Organisers say the bid builds on Denmark's cycling culture and its experience hosting major events, including Copenhagen Sprint and the 2019 Cyclocross World Championships in Bogense.

item.title: Leen Massaer: “Het is een echte meerwaarde om UCI-wedstrijden in België te hebben”
Future Racing - støt de cykelglade børn og unge under Copenhagen Sprint
item.description: Future Racing køres igen i år som en del af Copenhagen Sprint. Future Racing er et kort, intenst ungdomsløb på den lukkede omgang i København, som de professionelle også kører på både lørdag og søndag. Løbet giver børn og unge i aldersgrupperne U11-U17 fra vores cykelklubber muligheden for at opleve ægte løbsstemning, konkurrence og fællesskab i trygge rammer. Og efter sidste års succes håber vi i år at se endnu flere tilskuere langs ruten. Den ekstra opbakning fra publikum gør en kæmpe forskel for de unge ryttere og skaber en festlig ramme, som både motiverer og glæder deltagerne.Future Racing handler ikke kun om at komme først over stregen, men om at give næste generation af cykelryttere en unik oplevelse. De får oplevelsen af at køre på en lukket omgang i indre København med korte, eksplosive runder hvor tempo, teknik og nerver spiller sammen. For tilskuere er det en oplagt mulighed for at komme helt tæt på cykelsporten, følge kommende talenter i aktion og være med til at inspirere flere børn og unge til at kaste sig over sporten.Løbet køres søndag inden det professionelle felt rammer omgang. De første sendes ud på ruten kl. 10:30, så mød op i god tid, vælg et sted med god udsigt til mål eller et sving, og tag gerne familie og venner med - klapsalver og opmuntrende tilråb betyder meget for de unge ryttere. Husk at tjekke praktiske detaljer som starttider og ruteinformation på Sportstiming: www.sportstiming.dk/event/18537.Kom og støt de cykelglade børn og unge - din tilstedeværelse gør en stor forskel og er med til at skabe uforglemmelige oplevelser for fremtidens talenter.

item.title: Herrernes U23 DM i linjeløb - fremtidens stjerner sætter kurs mod Vejen
item.description: I morgen, lørdag den 6. juni, samles Danmarks bedste unge ryttere i Vejen, når Vejen Bicycle Club, Home Vejen og Vejen Kommune sammen med Danmark Cykle Union er værter for U23-herrernes DM i linjeløb 2026, hvor både start og mål er i hjertet af Vejen.U23 DM har historisk vist sig at være et godt pejlemærke for de fremtidig store danske cykelnavne. Øverst på podiet har tidligere stået navne som Rasmus Søjberg og Julius Johansen, og selv det næsthøjeste trin har vist sig at love godt: Cristopher Juul-Jensen, Magnus Cort og Casper Pedersen. Der er i høj grad lagt op til endnu et spændende U23 DM i linjeløb, og landstræner Michael Mørkøv ser frem til morgendagens løb med stor begejstring.”U23 DM er historisk set et mesterskab, hvor de største danske talenter viser sig frem. Det helt specielle ved U23-mesterskabet er, at de konkurrerer med jævnaldrende – i modsætning til elitens DM, hvor det kan være svært at sætte sit aftryk mod World Tour-rytterne.Jeg ser meget frem til løbet i Vejen i morgen, og som landstræner glæder jeg mig specielt til at se, hvem der viser sig frem.”Alfred Mørch Kongstad fra Airtox - Carl Ras har båret DM-trikoten det seneste år og er igen at finde på startstregen ved Museumspladsen i Vejen, når han skal forsøge at genvinde titlen. Det kan dog godt vise sig svært for den unge Airtox – Carl Ras-rytter, da Danmarks mest talentfulde U23-ryttere har meldt deres ankomst.Ruten byder på småkuperet terræn, der er langt vanskeligere i virkeligheden end på papiret - og vejret ser ud til at kunne spille en aktiv rolle lørdag eftermiddag, hvor det kan blæse op i løbet af dagen, mens regn kan gøre omgangene i Vejen teknisk svære.Bag løbet står Vejen Bicycle Club, der også er fast arrangør af spændende DEMIN Cup-afdelinger, støttet af Home Vejen og Vejen Kommune.11:40: Officiel start. 15:40: Forventet ankomst lille rundstrækning 16:00: Opløb 16:10 Præmieoverrækkelse

item.title: Landsholdene er klar til Copenhagen Sprint
item.description: Når der i weekenden den 13.-14. juni køres cykelløb på det allerhøjeste niveau på de sjællandske landeveje og københavnske gader, er det med landsholdet på startlisten. Copenhagen Sprint er klar til sin anden udgave og igen i år har landsholdet fået en plads i feltet, og nu har vores to landstrænere Mette Andersen og Michael Mørkøv sat deres hold.Når kvinderne ruller ud fra Roskilde lørdag med sigtet mod København, er det med en stærk gruppe i landsholdsdragten. Her er de kvinder, der er udtaget til landsholdet:Amalie DideriksenChristina Bragh LorenzenEllen KlingeLaura Auerbach-LindOlympia Norrid-MortensenSilje Weitze AntvorskovOg der er store ambitioner fra landstræner Mette Andersen, der glæder sig over World Tour-løbet på danske veje:”Vi glæder os helt vildt til lørdag den 13. juni. Det er utrolig vigtigt for kvindecyklingen, at vi har et World Tour-løb i Danmark – både for vores landshold, udviklingsarbejdet i hele Danmark og for kvindecyklingen generelt. Det er fantastisk at kunne vise alle pigerne ude i klubberne, at det faktisk er muligt at blive professionel cykelrytter som kvinde.” ”Vi kommer med et landshold, hvor vi både har erfaring og unge ryttere med stort potentiale. Sportsligt er det ambitionen at levere Amalie så frisk som muligt, når tingene skal afgøres. Omgangene i København bliver afgørende, så vores mål er, at vi som hold giver hende de bedste forudsætninger for at køre med om en topplacering.”Søndag går det løs for herrerne, der ligeledes tager turen fra Roskilde ind til København, og igen i år er det et utrolig stærkt felt. Her har Michael Mørkøv udtaget følgende ryttere til at køre i de danske farver:Alexander SalbyConrad HaugstedFrederik RodenbergMads AndersenRasmus Bøgh WallinSebastian NielsenTobias AagaardMichael Mørkøv har valgt syv stærke ryttere til søndagens Copenhagen Sprint, der skal være med til at skabe et spændende cykelløb på de sjællandske veje.”Det er en ret unik mulighed for nogle af vores største talenter og rutinerede ryttere at prøve sig af på World Touren – og så endda på hjemmebane i København.””Jeg glæder mig til, at vi som landshold kan sætte vores præg på løbet og give nogle af vores hjemlige ryttere en sjælden mulighed foran det danske publikum. Vi skal forsøge at måle os med verdenseliten, og det er en udfordring, vi ser frem til.”

item.title: Drie leiders houden stand in druk weekend Toekomstcup
item.description: Een druk weekeinde voor de deelnemers aan de Giant Schwalbe en Schwalbe Liv Toekomstcup afgelopen weekend. Eerst mochten de deelnemers een ploegentijdrit rijden op vrijdag in Nijeveen, waar zaterdag een omloop werd gehouden. Over beide onderdelen werd een klassement opgemaakt. Op zondag werd er individueel tegen de klok gereden in Kreileroord. Het zorgde voor de nodige verschuivingen in de Giant Schwalbe en Schwalbe Liv Toekomstcup.

item.title: Aktiv cykelweekend med landevej og mountainbike
item.description: Der var masser af aktivitet på tværs af dansk cykelsport i weekenden, hvor både første afdeling af Demin U19 Cup og tredje afdeling af Shimano MTB Ligaen blev afviklet.Elias Andersen tog sejren i Demin U19 CupI Fredericia blev første afdeling af Demin U19 Cup kørt, hvor nogle af landets stærkeste juniorryttere tørnede sammen.Det blev Elias Andersen fra Team Uno-X-Carl Ras Roskilde, der kunne række armene i vejret efter en stærk præstation og tage sejren i løbet. Alexander Louis Svenningsen fra CeramicSpeed Aros Forsikring HE sikrede sig andenpladsen, mens Vilfred Novak Nielsen fra Team Improv-Giant Store – CEC fuldendte podiet på tredjepladsen.Top 3:Elias Andersen (Team Uno-X-Carl Ras Roskilde)Alexander Louis Svenningsen (CeramicSpeed Aros Forsikring HE)Vilfred Novak Nielsen (Team Improv-Giant Store – CEC)Øvrige resultater kan ses på Sportstiming her: https://www.sportstiming.dk/event/17428/resultsShimano MTB Ligaen fortsatte i VardeSamtidig blev tredje afdeling af Shimano MTB Ligaen kørt i Varde, hvor rytterne blev udfordret på en varieret og teknisk rute.I herre elite var svenske Edvin Elofsson stærkest og tog sejren foran Kristian Østergaard Andersen og hjemmebanerytteren Tobias Bendix fra Varde Cykelklub.Hos kvinderne fortsatte Sara Aaboe Kallestrup de stærke takter og sikrede sig sejren foran Freja Dissing Andersen og Janni Spangsberg.EliteHerre EliteEdvin Elofsson (Serneke Allebike Cykelklubb)Kristian Østergaard Andersen (miKKen Cycling)Tobias Bendix (Varde Cykelklub)Kvinde EliteSara Aaboe Kallestrup (MTB Randers)Freja Dissing Andersen (Holte MTB Klub)Janni Spangsberg (Varde Cykelklub)Junior (U19)Hos juniorherrerne tog Johannes Brædstrup-Holm sejren foran Villads Nyegaard og Albert Lundgren.Hos juniorkvinderne blev det Caroline Matilde Hede, der kørte først over målstregen foran Rose-Mai Damsgaard og Lærke Dissing Andersen.UngdomsklasserneI U17-klassen vandt Malte Kempel Christensen foran Gabriel Wimmer Bøgeskov-Jensen og Kevin Corfitz Knudsen, mens Camilla Skovlod Kold tog sejren hos U17-pigerne.I U15 blev det Emil Oom, der vandt drengeklassen, mens Nanna Skamris var stærkest hos pigerne.I U13-klassen fortsatte Jonathan Oxholm Rasmussen de flotte resultater og tog endnu en sejr foran William Torbensen og Tobias Kjelsmark Nielsen. Hos pigerne vandt Maia Zeeberg Rod Kristensen.I U11-klassen gik sejren til Thorleif Tejlmand Rasmussen, mens Lara Sofie Düring Hansen tog sejren hos pigerne.Master- og motionsklasserDer blev også kørt stærkt i masterklasserne, hvor blandt andre Kasper Paulsen (H40), Michael Gamborg (H50), Bente Kortbæk (D50) og Palle Egeberg Jensen (H60) kunne lade sig hylde som vindere.Åbne klasserI den åbne 3 km-klasse tog Jonathan Oxholm Rasmussen endnu en sejr, mens Edvin Elofsson også viste sin styrke ved at vinde den åbne 4,9 km-klasse.Shimano MTB Ligaen fortsætter de kommende uger, hvor rytterne igen skal kæmpe om vigtige point i den samlede stilling.

De oud-prof van Picnic-PostNL versloeg Loes Nomden in de sprint met twee. Jillian Knook sprintte in het peloton naar een derde plaats. item.title: Toppers Kalas Clubcompetitie tonen zich in Vechtstede Omloop van Zwartewaterland
item.description: Sven Buskermolen is afgelopen zaterdag winnaar geworden van de Vechtstede Notarissen Omloop van Zwartewaterland. De renner van AW-Swabo reed in de wedstrijd uit de Kalas Clubcompetitie voor mannen in de slotkilometers weg uit een kopgroep van negen die in drie schuiven ontstond. Peter Schulting won de sprint van de achtervolgers, maar kon de Hanzerenners dichtbij huis geen zege bezorgen. Tim van der Poel werd derde in deze wedstrijd. Hij werd daarmee ook de leider in het jongerenklassement, terwijl Simon den Braber algemeen leider werd in de reeks. De Mol leidt bij de ploegen.

item.title: Algemene statutaire vergadering KBWB: aankondiging en definitieve agenda
Škoda BørneTour bliver en del af PostNord Danmark Rundt – cykelglæden rykker helt tæt på eliten
item.description: Når Danmarks største etapeløb til sommer ruller gennem landet, bliver det med ekstra fokus på fællesskab, oplevelser og cykelglæde for de yngste. Škoda BørneTour bliver i 2026 en integreret del af PostNord Danmark Rundt med events i udvalgte startbyer. Børn får dermed mulighed for selv at komme i sadlen og opleve suset fra et cykelløb på samme dag og i samme rammer som nogle af verdens bedste ryttere.Fire startbyer Škoda BørneTour afvikles i fire startbyer: · Aalborg den 29. juli www.sportstiming.dk/event/18600 · Glyngøre den 30. juli www.sportstiming.dk/event/18602 · Fredericia den 31. juli www.sportstiming.dk/event/18603 · Køge den 2. august www.sportstiming.dk/event/18604 Alle BørneTour-events starter umiddelbart efter at de professionelle ryttere er sendt afsted på dagens etape – så børnene kan overtage ruten og tilskuernes hyldest, og få deres egen store cykeloplevelse i trygge og afspærrede rammer. Events i forbindelse med PostNord DanmarkRundt er for børn i alderen 3-10 år. Det adskiller sig fra det normale BørneTour-format, som typisk henvender sig til børn og unge fra 3-15 år. Læs mere om de enkelte events på de respektive tilmeldingssider.Cykelglæde og fællesskab i centrum Formålet med Škoda BørneTour er ikke kun at inspirere fremtidens elitecykelryttere. Lige så vigtigt er det at skabe gode oplevelser med cyklen i centrum og invitere børn og familier ind i et stærkt fællesskab.Alle kan være med – uanset fart, cykeltype og ambitionsniveau. For nogle handler det om at køre stærkt og konkurrere, mens det for andre er en sjov dag med venner og familie.Med placeringen midt i PostNord Danmark Rundt får deltagerne samtidig mulighed for at opleve en ægte folkefest hvor cyklingen er omdrejningspunktet, og hvor stemningen fra det professionelle løb smitter af på børnenes egne løb.Tættere kobling mellem bredde og elite Med integrationen i PostNord Danmark Rundt styrkes forbindelsen mellem elitecykling og børne- og ungdomsniveauet og skaber en unik platform for inspiration.“Det her er en oplagt mulighed for at skabe endnu stærkere synergi mellem DCU’s events. PostNord Danmark Rundt er med til at inspirere fremtidens cykelryttere – som potentielt er blandt de børn der deltager i Škoda BørneTour. Når de oplever stemningen, ruten og fællesskabet på tæt hold, så håber vi på at det tænder en gnist og give lyst til selv at fortsætte i sporten,” siger DCU’s eventchef Klaus Falk Paarup. Alle BørneTour events afholdes i samarbejde med lokale B&U-cykelklubber, som i forlængelse af BørneTour står klar med introtilbud til interesserede børn og unge.Cykelsportens fremtid – og fællesskab – begynder her Samarbejdet markerer endnu et skridt i arbejdet med at få flere børn op på cyklen – ikke kun med fokus på talent, men på glæden ved at være en del af noget større.Med Škoda BørneTour som en del af sommerens cykelfest bliver det tydeligt: Der er plads til alle på cyklen.Hjemmeside: www.børnetour.dk Škoda BørneTour på facebook: www.facebook.com/boernetour Škoda BørneTour på Instagram: www.instagram.com/boernetour/

item.title: 436 Junioren woonden in maart en april de Gezondheidssessies bij
Stærke danske præstationer i international cykelweekend
item.description: Stærke danske resultater ved GP Framar på banenFra den 8.–10. maj blev GP Framar kørt på banen, hvor flere danskere markerede sig flot. I herre-eliteomnium blev Robin Skivild nummer 3, mens Oskar Winkler sluttede som nummer 17. Hos kvinderne leverede Laura Auerbach-Lind en stærk præstation og kørte sig til en flot 3. plads i omnium mens Anna Hansen kørte en flot 7. plads hjem. I pointløbet blev det til en 10. plads til Robin Skivild og en 20. plads til Oskar Winkler i herrernes eliteklasse, mens Laura Auerbach-Lind sluttede som nummer 8 og Anna Hansen som nummer 24 i kvindernes løb.I scratchløbet blev Robin Skivild nummer 12 og Oskar Winkler nummer 15, mens Laura Auerbach-Lind imponerede og tog sejren i kvindernes scratch og Laura Hansen fik en 11. plads i scratch.I herrernes madison leverede Oskar Winkler og Robin Skivild endnu en stærk præstation og kørte sig til en flot 2. plads.Dansk succes ved Course de la Paix JuniorsDet danske juniorlandshold var samtidig i aktion ved 54th Course de la Paix Juniors, hvor danskerne hentede flere flotte resultater.Malthe Risbjerg leverede et stærkt løb og sluttede som nummer 2 i det samlede klassement efter blandt andet at have vundet 2. etape. Julius Birkedal kørte sig ligeledes til en flot samlet 3. plads.Derudover leverede både Jakob Resen og Rasmus Helt Hansen solide præstationer gennem løbet.Medaljer og topplaceringer ved nordiske mesterskaber i MTBI Sverige blev de nordiske mesterskaber i mountainbike kørt, hvor de danske ryttere også markerede sig stærkt.I herre-eliten tog Sebastian Fini Juhl sejren, mens Caroline Bohé vandt dame-eliteklassen.I juniorherrerne blev Johannes Brædstrup-Holm nummer 2, Gustav Freitag Laursen nummer 3, Anton Hermann Nielsen nummer 4 og Villads Nyegaard nummer 5.Hos junior kvinderne kørte Caroline Matilde Hede sig til en flot 2. plads, mens Rose-Mai Damsgaard sluttede som nummer 5.I herrernes U23-klasse blev Hector Glud Hjorth nummer 8.Hos kvinder U23-klasse leverede danskerne en stærk samlet indsats. Julie Lillelund tog sejren, Freja Dissing Andersen blev nummer 2, Sara Aaboe Kallestrup nummer 4 og Smilla Zoffmann Glud nummer 6.Samlet set blev det endnu en weekend med stærke danske præstationer på tværs af discipliner og et flot billede på bredden i dansk cykelsport.

item.title: NATIONALE SELECTIES: NATIONS' CUP MEN JUNIORS - COURSE DE LA PAIX
Danskerne klar til Giro d’Italia 2026
item.description: Fredag den 8. maj går starten på årets Giro d’Italia, når feltet ruller ud til tre ugers hård kamp om den ikoniske lyserøde førertrøje.Årets udgave af Giroen starter i Bulgarien, hvor de første tre etaper skal køres, inden løbet bevæger sig videre mod Italien. Over de kommende tre uger skal rytterne tilbagelægge hele 3.468 kilometer og forcere imponerende 48.700 højdemeter.I alt stiller otte danske ryttere til start i årets Giro d’Italia – og for flere af dem bliver det en helt særlig debut.Hele fire af de danske ryttere skal nemlig køre Giro d’Italia for første gang, mens både Johan Price-Pejtersen og Niklas Larsen står foran deres første Grand Tour nogensinde.Danskere til startJonas Vingegaard (Team Visma | Lease a Bike) Tobias Lund Andresen (Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale) Rasmus Søjberg (Decathlon AG2R La Mondiale) Johan Price-Pejtersen (Alpecin-Deceuninck) Michael Valgren (EF Education-EasyPost) Christopher Juul-Jensen (Team Jayco AlUla) Niklas Larsen (Unibet Tietema Rockets) Mikkel Bjerg (UAE Team Emirates)For Jonas Vingegaard bliver det samtidig første gang, han stiller til start i Giro d’Italia, og det bliver spændende at følge danskeren gennem de tre krævende uger.Også ryttere som Mikkel Bjerg, Michael Valgren og Christopher Juul-Jensen bringer vigtig erfaring ind i løbet, mens flere af de yngre danske ryttere får mulighed for at opleve cykelsportens største scene for første gang.To særlige danske debuterDer bliver også dansk Grand Tour-debut til både Johan Price-Pejtersen og Niklas Larsen, som begge står foran deres første WorldTour-etapeløb på allerstørste scene.For Niklas Larsen bliver det et særligt skridt i karrieren. Larsen er et produkt af det danske banelandshold og har gennem flere år været en vigtig del af den danske succes i 4.000 meter holdforfølgelsesløb, hvor Danmark har hentet både medaljer og internationale topresultater.Nu venter en helt anden udfordring på landevejen, når han skal gennemføre tre hårde uger i Giro d’Italia.Også Johan Price-Pejtersen får sin Grand Tour-debut efter flere stærke præstationer på både enkeltstartscyklen og landevejen. For begge ryttere bliver Giroen en mulighed for at samle vigtig erfaring på allerhøjeste niveau.Tre uger med drama venterGiro d’Italia er kendt som et af cykelsportens hårdeste og mest uforudsigelige løb. Lange bjergafslutninger, tekniske nedkørsler, taktiske etaper og eksplosive finaler skaber hvert år masser af drama gennem de tre uger.Med otte danskere på startstregen er der lagt op til endnu en spændende Grand Tour set med danske briller

item.title: Save money, ditch the car and start cycling. Here’s what to do if it’s been a while
item.description: It feels like fuel prices can never get any higher, but somehow, they do. There is a way to help ease the strain - that bike you already have is a great way of getting you from A to B for cheap.

item.title: German Cycling
NK Veldrijden 2027 naar Gemert
item.description: Het Nederlands Kampioenschappen voor nieuwelingen, junioren en U23/elite zal in 2027 verreden worden in Gemert. Het wordt een bijzondere editie, want deze nationale titelstrijd zal volledig in het centrum van het Brabantse dorp plaatsvinden. Een echte centrumcross dus, met het iconische Kasteel Gemert als decor. De organisatie ligt in handen van R&TC Buitenlust.

item.title: Danske resultater ved WHOOP UCI MTB World Series
item.description: Årets første afdeling af WHOOP UCI MTB World Series blev i weekenden kørt i Sydkorea, hvor de danske ryttere fik en udfordrende start på sæsonen.Forholdene var krævende med et teknisk spor og svære betingelser, som stillede store krav til både teknik, udholdenhed og evnen til at tilpasse sig undervejs. På trods af dette leverede de danske ryttere flere solide præstationer på tværs af disciplinerne.Elite – Cross CountryI short track (XCC) blev det til en 16. plads for Sebastian Fini Juul, mens Simon Andreassen og Tobias Lillelund sluttede som henholdsvis nummer 34 og 35.I den olympiske disciplin (XCO) leverede Sebastian Fini Juhl et stærkt resultat med en 9. plads. Simon Andreassen blev nummer 27, mens Tobias Lillelund sluttede som nummer 53.Hos kvinderne kørte Sofie Heby Pedersen sig til en 15. plads i short track og en 32. plads i XCO.U23 – stærk dansk podieplaceringI U23-klassen var der særligt dansk succes i short track, hvor Gustav Heby Pedersen kørte sig til en flot 2. plads og sikrede sig en podieplacering.Mikkel Lose blev nummer 11, mens Nikolaj Hougs sluttede som nummer 15.I XCO-disciplinen blev Gustav Heby Pedersen nummer 18, Nikolaj Hougs nummer 20, mens Mikkel Lose desværre måtte udgå af løbet.Downhill – junior kvinderI downhill for junior kvinder leverede Rosa Mari Jensen en stærk præstation. Hun kvalificerede sig som nummer 2 og fulgte op med en flot 5. plads i finalen.

item.title: Nieuwe kansen voor Mountainbike-selectie tijdens Junior Series Heubach
item.description: Een echte klassieker op de kalender. Maar ook een eerlijke wedstrijd met lange klimmen en brede paden waarop genoeg kansen zijn om in te halen. Zo laat de HC wedstrijd in Heubach - tevens de volgende stop in de Junior Series - zich het beste omschrijven. Voor de KNWU selectie meteen een mooie kans om nog eens te laten zien wat ze in huis hebben. Helemaal met het oog op de eerste Europese wereldbeker mountainbiken in Nove Mesto een week later.

item.title: Schrijf je in voor het NK, EK en WK BMX Racing
item.description: Het is sinds 20 april mogelijk om je in te schrijven voor het NK, EK en WK BMX Racing 2026. Voldoe je aan de kwalificatie-eisen en wil je graag aan een van deze wedstrijden deelnemen? Vergeet dan niet je tijdig in te schrijven.

item.title: Check hier de pumptrackkalender 2026
item.description: Het komende seizoen staat er een goed gevulde pumptrackkalender klaar op MijnKNWU. Zo kan er voor de zomervakantie op maar liefst zes verschillende locaties gepumptrackt worden tijdens de Nationale Pumptrack Series (NPS), en staat de strijd om de nationale titel op 6 september in Oss op het programma tijdens het NK.

item.title: Grand Départ GB launches poster design competition for students
item.description: Art, design and creative students from across England, Scotland, and Wales have the chance to showcase their talents, with the opportunity to design the official posters for the historic 2027 Grand Départs of the Tour de France and Tour de France Femmes avec Zwift.

item.title: Teenager er en stor gave til Tour de France: 'Det er starten på en ny fortælling'
item.description: Det 19-årige stortalent Paul Seixas debuterer i Tour de France til sommer med ambitionen om at køre klassement.

item.title: Anden afdeling af Shimano MTB Ligaen kørt
item.description: I weekenden blev 2. afdeling af Shimano MTB Ligaen kørt i de tekniske spor i Rold Skov, hvor rytterne blev testet på både teknik, tempo og udholdenhed.Rold Skov bød på en klassisk MTB-rute med masser af sving, rødder og tempo – og selvom forholdene var krævende, blev der kørt stærkt på tværs af alle klasser. Både unge talenter og erfarne ryttere leverede flotte præstationer og skabte en dag med høj intensitet og god stemning i skoven.I de åbne klasser var der høj fart fra start. I 4,2 km-klassen tog Jonathan Oxholm Rasmussen (Hammel Cykle Klub) sejren foran William Torbensen (Aarhus MTB) og Tobias Kjelsmark Nielsen (Mountain Bike Klub Silkeborg).I 5,8 km-klassen var det Hector Guld Hjorth (Holte MTB Klub), der var stærkest, mens Kristian Østergaard Andersen (miKKen Cycling) og Paw Kragh (Racing Bjerringbro) fulgte efter.I ungdomsklasserne var der også flotte resultater. I U11 drenge tog Thorleif Tejlmand Rasmussen (Holte MTB Klub) sejren, mens Lara Sofia Düring Hansen (Mountainbike Club Vejle) vandt U11 piger.I U13-klassen gentog podiet fra 4,2 km-klassen sig med Jonathan Oxholm Rasmussen foran William Torbensen og Tobias Kjelsmark Nielsen. I U13 piger var det Maia Zeeberg Rod Kristensen (HMIF), der tog sejren foran Maya Holm-Denninger og Ellen Krogh Skammelsen.I U15 blev det Patrick Houlborg Jørgensen (MTB Randers), der kørte med sejren, mens Laura Filippa Posselt Gamborg (Hammel Cykle Klub) vandt U15 piger.I U17-klassen sejrede Malte Kempel Christensen (MTB Randers), mens Esther Mejdal Haaning (Mountainbike Club Vejle) var stærkest hos pigerne.I juniorrækkerne (U19) vandt Johannes Brædstrup-Holm (Holte MTB Klub) hos herrerne, mens Caroline Matilde Hede (Holte MTB Klub) tog sejren hos kvinderne.I eliteklasserne blev der kørt stærkt. Hos herrerne var det Hector Guld Hjorth (Holte MTB Klub), der tog sejren foran Kristian Østergaard Andersen og Paw Kragh. I kvindernes eliteklasse vandt Julie Lillelund (Mountainbike Club Vejle) foran Sara Aaboe Kallestrup (MTB Randers) og Freja Dissing Andersen (Holte MTB Klub).I B-klassen var Storm Siig Buus (Rold Skov MTB) stærkest, mens Jens Brokær (Hareskov Cykelklub) vandt H40. I D40 var Anne Mi Svarrer (Holte MTB Klub) eneste rytter og dermed vinder.I H50 vandt Michael Gamborg (Hammel Cykle Klub), mens Bente Kortbæk Kibsgaard (Viborg MTB) tog sejren i D50. I H60 var det Kåre Winther Hansen (Dansk Mountainbike Klub), der kørte først over stregen.Samlet set blev det endnu en stærk afdeling af Shimano MTB Ligaen med masser af fart, teknik og gode præstationer på tværs af alle klasser.

item.title: Dramatisk GP Herning og stærk dansk indsats
item.description: Weekenden bød på to af de største danske endagsløb med GP Herning og Fyen Rundt, hvor det danske landshold leverede stærke præstationer i begge løb.Kaotisk GP HerningGP Herning – ofte kaldt de danske Paris-Roubaix – levede endnu en gang op til sit ry som et hårdt og uforudsigeligt løb.Det danske landshold var særligt aktivt i front, hvor både Kristian Egholm og Gustav Wang satte deres præg på løbet.Egholm kørte sig i et stærkt soloudbrud og opbyggede et forspring på omkring 40 sekunder. Løbet tog dog en dramatisk drejning, da feltet blev sendt forkert på ruten, hvilket førte til en neutralisering og efterfølgende genstart af løbet – en situation, der desværre gik ud over Egholms fordel.Efter genstarten kæmpede Egholm sig tilbage i løbet, men blev hentet med omkring 40 kilometer til mål af en mindre gruppe, hvor også Gustav Wang sad med.I finalen var det Wang, der holdt sig fremme og kørte sig med i afgørelsen. Han endte i en spurt mod Mads Landbo (ColoQuick), hvor Landbo trak det længste strå.Top 3 – GP Herning:Mads Landbo (ColoQuick)Gustav Wang (Danmarks landshold)Frederik Gustav Dahl (Give Steel)Fyen Rundt – stærk form fra WangDagen efter fortsatte de gode danske takter i Fyen Rundt, hvor landsholdet igen var synligt i front.Endnu en gang var Gustav Wang blandt de mest offensive ryttere og viste stærk form gennem hele weekenden. Han kom med i et udbrud med omkring 10 ryttere, som senere blev reduceret til en mindre frontgruppe.Ind mod finalen forsøgte Wang at sætte et afgørende angreb, men blev hentet igen kort før målstregen. Selvom det ikke blev til en topplacering, var det endnu en stærk indsats fra landsholdsrytteren.Top 3 – Fyen Rundt:Mads Andersen (Swatt Club)Sebastian Nielsen (Team ColoQuick)Pelle Køster (Airtox-Carl Ras)Samlet set blev det en weekend med masser af drama, høj intensitet og stærke danske præstationer – særligt fra Gustav Wang, der markerede sig som en af de mest formstærke ryttere over begge løb.

item.title: Leiders naar de winst in Drentse dubbel
item.description: Zowel Bo Kramer (nieuwelingen), Splinter van ’t Hoff (junioren) als Britt Nederlof (nieuweling-meisjes) gaven hun leiderstrui in de Giant Schwalbe en de Schwalbe Liv Toekomstcup extra kleur door als klassementsleider te winnen in de Omloop Door het Land van Kleine Hein. En de winnares bij de junior-vrouwen, de Deense Ida Falla, werd na de overwinning in Noord-Sleen ook als leidster gehuldigd. Bij de nieuwelingen (m/v) werd er gesprint met een grote groep voor de overwinning, bij de junioren (m/v) waren het de aanvallers die wisten te winnen.

item.title: Gezocht: organisator NK Mountainbike 2027!
item.description: Lijkt het jouw club of vereniging gaaf om het middelpunt van de mountainbikesport te zijn in 2027? Stel je dan kandidaat voor het organiseren van de Nederlandse Kampioenschappen Mountainbike XCC, XCO en Jeugd! Dit jaar wordt dit combinatie-evenement voor het eerst georganiseerd in Honselersdijk bij Westland Wil Vooruit. Deze opzet geldt als blauwdruk voor de nationale kampioenschappen in de komende jaren.

item.title: Schrijf je in voor de MTB Streetrace-competitie 2026!
item.description: Het mountainbike-seizoen is in volle gang: inmiddels zijn in de LJC en NL Cup alweer een aantal wedstrijden verreden, en ook de 3 Nations Cups op Nederlandse bodem zijn inmiddels achter de rug. Hoog tijd dus, om met de streetrace-competitie 2026 van start te gaan! Gelukkig duurt dat niet lang meer, want vanaf juni is Drenthe opnieuw het decor van de wedstrijdserie in deze snelle mountainbike-discipline.

item.title: Danskere til start i WHOOP UCI MTB World Cup
item.description: Flere danske ryttere er i aktion, når årets WHOOP UCI MTB World Cup skydes i gang i Sydkorea.World Cup-serien består af i alt 14 løb hen over sæsonen, hvor verdens bedste mountainbikeryttere konkurrerer i forskellige discipliner fra fredag til søndag.Selvom rytterne stiller til start for deres respektive hold – og ikke for Danmark – er der alligevel stærk dansk repræsentation i flere klasser.Herre EliteSimon Andreassen Sebastian Fini Juul Tobias LillelundDame EliteSofie Heby PedersenU23 HerreGustav Heby Pedersen Nikolaj Hougs Mikkel LoseCaroline Bohé skulle også have været med, men blev desværre syg op til afrejse og måtte blive hjemme.Held og lykke til alle de danske atleter i weekenden!

item.title: Het trial-seizoen 2026 gaat van start!
item.description: Zondag 3 mei staat alweer de eerste wedstrijd in de nationale trial series op het programma! De aftrap van deze competitie vindt plaats in Zelhem. Anderhalve maand later kunnen de rijders zich alweer opmaken voor het NK en de tweede wedstrijd in de Dutch Trial Series in Diphoorn op 28 juni. Na de zomervakantie wordt de competitie vervolgd in Schijndel op 13 september, en op 10 oktober afgesloten in Nunspeet.

item.title: Directeur-Bestuurder Maurice Leeser neem afscheid van KNWU
item.description: Maurice Leeser vertrekt na drieënhalf jaar als directeur-bestuurder van de KNWU. Leeser neemt afscheid tijdens de ledenraad op 17 juni, waar Andreas Roodbeen, manager bedrijfsvoering, wordt voorgedragen als interim-directeur-bestuurder.

item.title: Udtaget: Truppen til GP Herning og Fyen Rundt
item.description: Landstræner Michael Mørkøv har udtaget truppen til weekendens to danske løb – GP Herning den 2. maj og Fyen Rundt den 3. maj.Weekenden byder på to løb, der stiller forskellige krav til rytterne – fra de åbne veje omkring Herning til de mere kuperede og taktisk krævende ruter på Fyn. Med udsigt til godt vejr er der lagt op til høj fart og offensiv kørsel fra start.Begge løb er kendt for at skabe selektion undervejs og stiller store krav til både positionering, samarbejde og evnen til at læse løbet.GP Herning – 02.05.2026Henrik Breiner Pedersen Alexander Salby Gustav Wang Kristian Egholm Kevin Biehl Johan EltoftFyen Rundt – 03.05.2026Henrik Breiner Pedersen Alexander Salby Gustav Wang Kristian Egholm Kevin Biehl Noah AndersenDer er dermed lagt op til en spændende weekend med danske ryttere i aktion på hjemmebane.

item.description: Retrouvez le direct avec GPS tracking du Tour de Romandie 2026.

item.title: Škoda BørneTour skudt i gang
item.description: Lørdag blev sæsonen for Škoda BørneTour skudt i gang i Tårnby, hvor blæsten satte sit præg på dagen. På trods af den stride vind mødte mange børn op med højt humør og masser af cykelglæde.Langs ruten var der god stemning, og både deltagere og tilskuere var med til at skabe en festlig start på årets tour. Børn i alle aldre gav den gas på cyklerne og viste, at det vigtigste ikke nødvendigvis er at komme først – men at være med og have det sjovt.Škoda BørneTour er et cykelløb for børn i alderen 3–15 år og henvender sig til både nybegyndere og mere erfarne unge ryttere. Løbene køres på korte og sikre rundstrækninger i byer over hele landet fra maj til september.Fokus er på fællesskab, bevægelse og cykelglæde – og alle kan være med uanset fart og ambitionsniveau.Škoda BørneTour kommer rundt i hele landet, og hvis man ikke havde mulighed for at deltage i Tårnby, er der rig mulighed for at være med næste gang. Allerede næste stop er i Middelfart, hvor løbet også bliver en del af cykelfesten omkring Fyen Rundt.Alle events kan findes her, hvor det også er muligt at tilmelde sig: https://www.sportstiming.dk/bornetour/eventsDer er dermed lagt op til endnu en sæson fyldt med cykelglæde, fællesskab og gode oplevelser på tværs af landet.

item.title: ‘I Was Given a 1% Chance of Survival, Now I’m on the saddle’ - The experience at a British Cycling Para-cycling Talent ID Day
item.description: British Cycling run annual Para-cycling Talent Identification days across the country, searching for the next generation of athletes to enter our performance pathway. This year’s first session took place at Derby Arena, where disabled riders were tested across a range of physical challenges designed to uncover raw potential.
